mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when the unemployment rate is below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression, or the signal fails when annual CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing and utility output, which improves short-term corporate profit margins and inventory turnover, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon during early economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with industrial production changes when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic momentum and rising household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and corporate earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth is driven by temporary government hiring, or forward returns turn negative in greater than 50 percent of instances when accompanied by rising interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion, which increases aggregate corporate revenues and improves equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows, or GDP growth fails to translate into equity gains during stagflationary periods characterized by rising inflation and slowing output.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized strength across manufacturing and construction sectors, reducing recession risk and supporting equity risk appetite, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes when housing starts are driven by speculative bubbles, or the signal fails to predict returns when industrial production growth is negative for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls during late-cycle expansions signals an impending economic contraction, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Signal produces false negatives during early-cycle labor market adjustments, or 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances across multiple market cycles.
